attrs.content: What is the sign for "weather" in American Sign Language (ASL)?
attrs.name: description

text_preview: "weather" American Sign Language (ASL) ASL Lessons | Bookstore | Library | ASL University Main ► WEATHER: The American Sign Language (ASL) sign for "weather" The sign for "weather" has a few popular versions. The main version uses "W" hands and moves them both downward using a wavy movement. In a way, this is actually an initialized version of the signs for "snow" and "rain" which also both move downward. WEATHER: Use a wavy downward movement. Non-initialized version: Sample sentence: Do you like hot weather? Here is another version of weather. It means the same as the two handed version. Use a wavy downward movement. WEATHER: (version 3) In a message dated 10/5/2007 7:51:28 A.M. Pacific Daylight Time, mtew@ writes: Hi again!!
